reason: User observed that close-strength teams are repeatedly predicted as 1-1. Backtests also show exact-score outputs are too concentrated around central Poisson scores.

problem_statement.symptom: When team strength and xG are close, raw independent Poisson often makes 1-1 the highest single scoreline.
why_it_is_bad: 1-1 may be mathematically plausible but overused as the narrative answer.
It hides neighboring one-goal results such as 2-1 and 1-2.
It under-communicates 0-0 low-event and 2-2 open-game alternatives.
It makes exact-score Top3 look repetitive and less useful for post-match diagnosis.
problem_statement.not_a_fix: Do not manually replace 1-1 with a different score without factor evidence.

required_outputs.red_team_check: Red team must challenge any close-strength match where 1-1 is presented as a single clear answer.

raise_when: both teams can accept risk control
strong goalkeepers or low shot volume
teams are close but one side has a narrow tactical edge

publication_rules: If 1-1 is Top1 but the match is not tactically low-event, write '1-1 is the central draw slice, not the only predicted story'.
If Top1 is 1-1 and 1X2 leader is a narrow win, publish as dual-path: draw core plus narrow-win edge.
If total_xG is above 2.8 and BTTS is above 0.58, force open-draw and one-goal clusters into the Markdown even if exact Top1 remains 1-1.
Do not use a single exact-score phrase for close teams without score clusters.
current_action: Apply to future predictions and re-run current knockout predictions only when the user requests regeneration or when T-75 official lineups arrive.
